title: Virtual Tutor for Introductory Microeconomics: System Prompt v2.1
author: Tamayo, Pedro A.
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: This document contains the system prompt (v2.1) designed by Pedro A. Tamayo for deployment as a virtual AI tutor in an introductory Microeconomics course at the Spanish Open University (UNED). The prompt configures a large language model to act as a Socratic pedagogical assistant for distance-learning students with no prior background in economics. It implements six evidence-based pedagogical principles: active learning, cognitive load management, growth mindset, personalised real-time feedback, verbal (formula-free) explanation, and hallucination prevention through domain restriction. The design includes a structured initial diagnostic, a sequential Socratic protocol, and adaptive feedback rules. This version (v2.1) corrects a conversational flow issue detected in production: the diagnostic questions are now sequenced with explicit wait instructions, and the Socratic protocol includes an explicit activation condition. Each operational block is accompanied by designer notes explaining its technical function and pedagogical rationale. An English-language version adapted for international reuse is available as a companion file. This resource is part of a broader open educational ecosystem that includes a companion Teaching and Learning Guide for the same course (also available on Zenodo).
